channel.title: Kyverno on Shubham Singh
channel.description: Recent content in Kyverno on Shubham Singh

item.title: Introduction to Kubernetes Native Policy Management with Kyverno
item.description: Kyverno is a dynamic admission controller that can be used to govern and enforce how a Kubernetes cluster is used. Admission Controllers Let’s first start with admission controllers since Kyverno is an admission controller. In Kubernetes admission controllers are used to intercept requests going to the API servier and one of the two or both the things with them - mutate or/and validate. Kubernetes comes up with built-in admission controllers. The issue with these built-in admission controllers is that they are highly specific and different organizations may have different use cases with admission contollers not being fulfilled by these built-in admission controllers, therefore, for meeting these requirements we have dynamic admission controllers that can be developed as extensions and can be run as webhooks configured at runtime.

item.title: Open-source Contributor (CNCF)
item.description: Outline Actively contributed to multiple Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) projects for over 3 years, working across codebases, testing, and documentation. Collaborated closely with project maintainers to identify and fix critical issues, improve developer experience, and strengthen overall project quality. Contributions spanned engineering, documentation, and introducing projects to a wider audience through videos and presentations. Fixed 13 critical issues in CNCF projects, including improving webhook-CLI consistency, standardizing image variable fields, creating less vulnerable production-level image, enhancing error logging and increasing test coverage; authored 13 Pull Requests that got merged.
